mission.title: Install and Configure Open WebUI
mission.description: Open WebUI is a user-friendly AI platform designed for self-hosting, supporting various LLM runners like Ollama and OpenAI-compatible APIs. It offers seamless integration and extensibility, making it ideal for developers looking to deploy AI solutions offline. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and management of resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Open WebUI CLI tool to facilitate the deployment process.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

```

> This command downloads the CLI for Linux. Adjust for your OS if necessary.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/releases/download/v0.8.8/open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv open-webui-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/open-webui

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create open-webui-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials open-webui-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Open WebUI
steps.description:
Deploy Open WebUI using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com

helm install open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui --create-namespace

```

> Add the Open WebUI Helm repository.
> This command installs Open WebUI in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Open WebUI deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/k8s/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/open-webui/open-webui/main/k8s/monitoring.yaml

```

> This applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Open WebUI
uninstall.description:
Remove Open WebUI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related data and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall open-webui --namespace open-webui

kubectl delete namespace open-webui

```

> This command uninstalls the Open WebUI release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Open WebUI
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

helm upgrade open-webui open-webui/open-webui --version 0.8.8 --namespace open-webui

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

```

> Backup current resources before upgrading.
> Upgrade Open WebUI to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n open-webui -o yaml > open-webui-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:8080: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly set up and that the cluster is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install Open WebUI: chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm repository may not be added correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-add the Helm repository using 'helm repo add open-webui https://charts.openwebui.com' and update with 'helm repo update'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n open-webui' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the Open WebUI deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM models, improved user interface, and enhanced security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Open WebUI dashboard accessible at http://<your-cluster-ip>:<port> with all components running smoothly.
